title: Standard score
description: How many standard deviations apart from the mean an observed datum is
extract: In statistics, the standard score or z-score is the number of standard deviations by which the value of a raw score is above or below the mean value of what is being observed or measured. Raw scores above the mean have positive standard scores, while those below the mean have negative standard scores.
extract_html: In statistics, the standard score or z-score is the number of standard deviations by which the value of a raw score is above or below the mean value of what is being observed or measured. Raw scores above the mean have positive standard scores, while those below the mean have negative standard scores.
